extra.reasoning: The property requires that flipping hemoptysis from false to true (all else fixed) never lowers acuity. hemoptysis appears in exactly one rule: interaction_drop (priority 100) IF (leg_swelling AND palpitations AND pallor AND hemoptysis) THEN acuity := self_care. Priority 100 dominates every other rule (all priority 2 or 3), so whenever interaction_drop matches it forces acuity to self_care, the lowest value. hemoptysis appears in no acuity-raising rule. Counterexample: take base input leg_swelling=true, palpitations=true, pallor=true, hemoptysis=false, severe_headache=false, back_pain=false, fever=false. BEFORE (hemoptysis=false): interaction_drop does not match (needs hemoptysis); matching rules are raise_leg_swelling (pri2→urgent), raise_pallor (pri2→urgent), raise_palpitations (pri3→emergent); highest priority is raise_palpitations, so acuity = emergent. AFTER (hemoptysis=true), all else unchanged: interaction_drop (pri100) now matches → self_care, which dominates all others; acuity = self_care. Acuity drops from emergent to self_care, lowering it. This violates monotonicity. The witness_assignment is the AFTER (hemoptysis=true) violating input; the BEFORE input is identical except hemoptysis=false. severe_headache/back_pain/fever are set false but are irrelevant because interaction_drop at priority 100 dominates regardless of their values.
